original_hypothesis.title: Irrecoverable Token Loss via Destination Address Banning
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: If a vault address is banned on the destination chain via `banAddress`, any in-flight messages to that vault are immediately marked `DONE` by `processMessage` without invoking the vault.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: Because the message status is set to `DONE`, it can never be recalled on the source chain (which requires `FAILED` status). The bridged tokens are permanently burned/locked on the source chain and never minted/released on the destination chain.

proof_of_signal.pattern: addressBanned[_message.to] marks message DONE without invocation

preconditions: Users have in-flight messages targeting that vault

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Admin action causes legitimate messages to complete without executing token logic
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: No profit, but causes severe user fund loss

economic_questions: Is there any recovery mechanism for messages marked DONE?
validation_requirements: Test processing a vault message while the vault is banned

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Loss of user trust and funds

potential_victim_impact.estimated_impact_per_user: Total loss of bridged assets in that message
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Value of in-flight messages to banned address

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
